instructions.instruction: Command files go in `.opencode/command/<name>.md` (project) or `~/.config/opencode/command/<name>.md` (global). The filename is the slash command name.
Use `$ARGUMENTS` to insert the user-provided arguments into the command template. For positional args use `$1`, `$2`.
original_snippets: For commands that need arguments (e.g. `/review src/api.ts`): `$ARGUMENTS`

instructions.instruction: Use `!` prefix for shell command injection (e.g. `!git log --oneline -10`). Use `@` for file content injection.
instructions.relevant_when: when commands need dynamic runtime context

instructions.instruction: NEVER instruct an agent to invoke slash commands — agents cannot type /commands. Use shell commands directly instead.
original_snippets: Agents cannot invoke slash commands. Commands are user-to-agent shortcuts only.

instructions.instruction: Only use `subtask: true` for fire-and-forget tasks. Do NOT use it for interactive or multi-step workflows.
original_snippets: Only use `subtask: true` for fire-and-forget tasks that produce a single final output.
